metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_440
metadata.comment:
Rationale: A PKA RII alpha knockout is used to study the role of RII alph in synaptic plasticity. In this mouse line, a reduction in LTP is observed in layer II/III of the visual cortex.

The exact mechanism by which PKAs mediate LTP are unknown. In the hippocampus, PKA mediated phosphorylation of GluR1 is essential for AMPA receptor trafficking (Man et.al.), but it is unkown whether this is also required in LTP in the visual cortex.. Experimental description: RIIα knockout mice were generated on a 129 Sv/J background and bred on a C57BL/6 background (Burton et al., 1997). Experiments were performed blind to genotype. Genotypes were confirmed by PCR (Fig. 1A).
